root_cause_summary: The User model's email field is named 'email', but repository.py line 62 incorrectly accesses 'email_address' attribute, causing AttributeError when get_user_email_domain is called.

candidate_targets.rationale: Exact traceback location, contains the bug on line 62 where 'email_address' is used instead of 'email', excerpt shows comment indicating bug awareness.
candidate_targets.change_summary: Change user.email_address to user.email on line 62

candidate_targets.rationale: Contains test for get_user_email_domain method, should verify the fix works correctly and doesn't break existing tests.
candidate_targets.change_summary: Ensure test_get_email_domain_success passes with the corrected attribute name

repair_plan: 2. Remove the '# type: ignore[attr-defined]' comment as it will no longer be needed
3. Run existing tests to verify the fix doesn't break other functionality
validation_focus: Syntax: Ensure no syntax errors after attribute name change
Type checking: Verify mypy/pyright no longer flags the attribute access
Test coverage: Run test_repository.py tests, especially TestRepositoryEmailDomain.test_get_email_domain_success
Edge cases: Test with users having no email, malformed emails, or None users
additional_notes: The models.py file shows User model has 'email' field, confirming the correct attribute name
The repository.py excerpt contains a comment 'BUG: Using wrong attribute name' which strongly indicates this is the issue
No other files need changes as the bug is localized to a single line in repository.py
